_comment: Job-type -> Claude model routing for Veditor Studio. server.py should classify the job (reuse _classify_job + the prompt-prefix markers), look up the model here, and pass it to --model. Anything not listed falls through to `default`. Rationale: copywriting/deterministic-render jobs do not need Opus-level reasoning; only genuine multi-step planning/review does. See COST_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN.md.

prompt_markers._comment: Prefix strings to detect job type from the prompt head (uppercased), mirroring _classify_job. STREAMLINED AD is added because the current classifier misses it and those jobs fall to 'chat'/Opus.
prompt_markers.CAPTION: captions

notes.copy_offload: When copy_gen.py (local Ollama) is wired in, variants/streamlined/hook stop needing Claude for the wordsmithing at all — Claude Code just orchestrates and can run on Haiku.
notes.override: A per-request `model` from the UI should still win over this map, so power users can force Opus when they want it.
notes.opus_reserved_for: genuine multi-step reasoning, cross-file review, and motion-graphics design work — not deterministic renders or copywriting.
